parentQuestion: The connective tissue that stores fat is called ___.

scaffolds.q: The body stores energy for later use.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! When you eat more calories than you burn, the excess is stored for times when food is scarce.

scaffolds.q: Fat (triglycerides) is the body's main long-term energy storage molecule.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Fat contains more than twice the energy per gram compared to carbohydrates. Very efficient storage!

scaffolds.q: Specialized cells store fat in large droplets that fill most of the cell.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Fat cells (adipocytes) are mostly filled with one giant lipid droplet - the nucleus gets pushed to the edge.

scaffolds.q: The tissue made of fat-storing cells is called ADIPOSE tissue.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! 'Adipo-' means fat. Adipose tissue = fat tissue.

scaffolds.q: Adipose tissue is classified as connective tissue.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Despite being mostly fat droplets, adipose tissue is a type of connective tissue because of its developmental origin and matrix.

scaffolds.q: Adipose tissue does MORE than just store fat - it also secretes hormones.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! Adipose tissue is an endocrine organ. It releases leptin (satiety signal) and other hormones that affect metabolism.

scaffolds.q: Adipose tissue also provides insulation and cushioning for organs.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Fat under your skin insulates against cold, and fat around kidneys protects them from impact.

scaffolds.q: ADIPOSE tissue stores fat in specialized cells called adipocytes.
scaffolds.explain: Correct! These cells are designed for fat storage - each is essentially a giant triglyceride droplet with a rim of cytoplasm.

scaffolds.q: The answer is ADIPOSE tissue - the connective tissue specialized for fat storage.
scaffolds.explain: Yes! Not areolar (loose packing tissue), not dense connective tissue (tendons/ligaments). Adipose = fat storage tissue.
